figures.caption: Figure 5: Defense comparison on spatial across ε ∈ {2, 4, 8, 16}/255. Left: realized Robraw (unclamped) vs. ε, ±1 std over 3 seeds. Right: encoder-PCA channel ceiling Sdef enc vs. ε. The two panels are orthogonal axes (mechanism diagnostic vs. realized robustness); see Sec. 5.6 for the ordering and the +41 to +101 nats input-side shift.

Figure 4: Cumulative bound vs. horizon T on spatial ε = 8, ntraj = 30. Top: RHST matches the T · S1 reference within 0.03%, confirming Cor. 3. Bottom: LHST stays inside the MINE small-N band, four orders below RHS.

Figure 3: OpenVLA-7B on LIBERO. Left: realized robustness Rob vs. attack budget ε across 48 cells (4 suites × 4 budgets × 3 seeds); color and marker shape both encode suite, lighter dots are per-seed values, lines connect the per-ε mean within each suite. The Rob ∈ [−1.2,−0.1] band is the MINE small-N sign artifact. Right: per-suite bound terms at ε = 8/255 using the PCA-tightened RHS (Table 3); RHS exceeds LHS by ∼ 3–4 orders of magnitude.
Figure 2: P7 Gaussian-VLA: median slack vs. ε across 252 cells; bands show IQR. Both Sa and Sm stay non-negative and decrease monotonically (Cor. 1).
